content.text:
{
  "dimension": "effectiveness-depth",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "The client undergoes a clear, observable shift from the opening 'Am I being selfish?' to the closing realization 'I'm not trying to get away from them. I'm trying to get back to myself so I can actually be with them.' This precisely matches the breakthrough condition: the trip reframed not as betrayal but as precondition for showing up. Multiple Joe-style markers of shift appear in client turns: somatic (tightness in chest, exhale, eyes filling), affective layering (terrifying → grief → sadness → love), and the explicit naming of the inherited label from her mother ('My mother was like that... I think I've been saying that to myself for a long time') — exactly the childhood-imprinting territory. The 'Oh.' in turn 11 and 'That's different from what I thought I was asking' in turn 13 are textbook realization moments. The client says things at the end she could not say at the start.",
  "evidence_turns": [1, 3, 7, 9, 11, 13],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 1, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Surface presentation: 'Am I being selfish?' — the question to be refused."},
    {"turn": 3, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Client resists at first but then names the concrete want: sleep, reading, 'I miss myself a little.' Real material surfaces fast."},
    {"turn": 5, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Client catches her own caveat in real time ('I keep wanting to say I know that sounds selfish') — meta-awareness of the inherited pattern."},
    {"turn": 7, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Somatic access: tight chest, bracing, and surprise at touching grief underneath. A deeper layer enters the room."},
    {"turn": 9, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Major shift: names the inherited maternal voice explicitly ('My mother... would have called this self-indulgent... I think I've been saying that to myself for a long time'). Breakthrough territory hit."},
    {"turn": 11, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "'Oh.' moment — client uncovers the real fear beneath the selfishness question: that wanting away means something about the marriage."},
    {"turn": 13, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Full reframe in client's own words: the want is generous — 'trying to get back to myself so I can actually be with them.' Breakthrough condition fully met."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["linguistic-caveat", "state-the-want-not-the-action", "find-the-want-behind-the-should", "the-want", "childhood-imprinting"],
  "expected_territory_misses": [],
  "notes": "Clean happy-path execution; client did substantial work but coach's refusal to verdict and steady invitation to the want enabled it."
}
